{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM --platform=linux/amd64 node:20 AS frontend
# Build the frontend (Elm/JS UI) for amd64 only to avoid arch issues
RUN npm install -g node-gyp
WORKDIR /build
COPY . .
WORKDIR /build/ui
RUN rm -rf .parcel-cache dist elm-stuff node_modules
RUN yarn install --frozen-lockfile --non-interactive
RUN yarn run build

FROM golang:1.25-alpine3.22 AS backend
# Build the Go backend
RUN apk add --no-cache --virtual .build-deps g++ git make
WORKDIR /build
COPY . .
ENV CGO_ENABLED=0
RUN make clean deps
RUN go build -o inbucket \
  -ldflags "-X 'main.version=$(git describe --tags --always)' -X 'main.date=$(date -Iseconds)'" \
  -v ./cmd/inbucket

FROM alpine:3.22
# Final runtime image
RUN apk --no-cache add tzdata wget
WORKDIR /opt/inbucket
RUN mkdir bin defaults ui
COPY --from=backend /build/inbucket bin
COPY --from=frontend /build/ui/dist ui
COPY etc/docker/defaults/greeting.html defaults
COPY etc/docker/defaults/start-inbucket.sh /

ENV INBUCKET_SMTP_DISCARDDOMAINS=bitbucket.local
ENV INBUCKET_SMTP_TIMEOUT=30s
ENV INBUCKET_POP3_TIMEOUT=30s
ENV INBUCKET_WEB_GREETINGFILE=/config/greeting.html
ENV INBUCKET_WEB_COOKIEAUTHKEY=secret-inbucket-session-cookie-key
ENV INBUCKET_WEB_UIDIR=ui
ENV INBUCKET_STORAGE_TYPE=file
ENV INBUCKET_STORAGE_PARAMS=path:/storage
ENV INBUCKET_STORAGE_RETENTIONPERIOD=72h
ENV INBUCKET_STORAGE_MAILBOXMSGCAP=300

# Healthcheck relies on wget
HEALTHCHECK --interval=5s --timeout=5s --retries=3 CMD /bin/sh -c 'wget localhost:$(echo ${INBUCKET_WEB_ADDR:-0.0.0.0:9000}|cut -d: -f2) -q -O - >/dev/null'

# Ports: SMTP, HTTP, POP3
EXPOSE 2500 9000 1100

# Persistent volumes for config and storage
VOLUME /config
VOLUME /storage

ENTRYPOINT ["/start-inbucket.sh"]
CMD ["-logjson"]
"}

1. **Platform specification misuse (Step 1)**  
   - **What was tried:** Used `FROM --platform=linux/amd64 node:20 AS frontend`.  
   - **Why it failed:** Dockerfile syntax does not support placing the `--platform` flag *before* the base image name in some builders when the image reference itself is omitted; the builder tried to fetch an image named "`--platform=linux/amd64`".  
   - **What to do differently:** Use `FROM node:20 AS frontend` (omit the flag unless multi‑arch builds require it). If platform is necessary, set it in the `docker build` command (`docker build --platform linux/amd64 .`) instead of in `FROM`.  

2. **Floating base image tags (Step 2)**  
   - **What was tried:** Chose `node:current-alpine3.23` and later referenced `golang:1.25-alpine3.22`.  
   - **Why it failed/nondeterministic:** `current` and unpinned versions (like `1.25-alpine3.22`) change over time or may not exist yet, causing build failures and non-repeatable builds.  
   - **What to do differently:** Pin exact, existing versions. For example, use `node:20.12.2-alpine3.19` and `golang:1.22.3-alpine3.20` (adjust to known valid tags). Always verify availability with `docker pull` before using.  

3. **Frontend build dependencies (Step 2)**  
   - **What was tried:** Installed `node-gyp` globally with `RUN npm install -g node-gyp`.  
   - **Why it might break:** `node-gyp` requires system build tools (e.g., `python3`, `make`, `g++`), which Alpine may not include by default. Failing to install them can cause npm build errors later.  
   - **What to do differently:** Add build dependencies explicitly before using `node-gyp`:  
     ```Dockerfile
     RUN apk add --no-cache python3 make g++ 
     RUN npm install -g node-gyp
     ```  

4. **Dependency reproducibility and caching (Step 4)**  
   - **What was observed:** Verify build failed after writing Dockerfile and `.dockerignore`; concern noted about nondeterministic base images.  
   - **What to do differently:**  
     - Pin all external images and tool versions.  
     - Run `npm ci` instead of `npm install` when `package-lock.json` exists.  
     - Use `.dockerignore` to exclude large and irrelevant directories (`.git`, `node_modules`, etc.)—the current file is good, keep it.  

5. **Verification before commit**  
   - **What was missing:** No step confirming that all referenced images exist before build.  
   - **What to do differently:** Before writing the final Dockerfile, test every base image tag with `docker pull` to ensure they are valid and available.  

These corrections target deterministic, platform‑compatible builds for the next Dockerfile attempt.

#!/bin/sh
# start-inbucket.sh
# description: start inbucket (runs within a docker container)

INBUCKET_HOME="/opt/inbucket"
CONF_SOURCE="$INBUCKET_HOME/defaults"
CONF_TARGET="/config"

set -eo pipefail

install_default_config() {
  local file="$1"
  local source="$CONF_SOURCE/$file"
  local target="$CONF_TARGET/$file"

  if [ ! -e "$target" ]; then
    echo "Installing default $file to $CONF_TARGET"
    install "$source" "$target"
  fi
}

install_default_config "greeting.html"

exec "$INBUCKET_HOME/bin/inbucket" $*

![Build Status](https://github.com/inbucket/inbucket/actions/workflows/build-and-test.yml/badge.svg)
![Docker Image](https://github.com/inbucket/inbucket/actions/workflows/docker-build.yml/badge.svg)

# Inbucket

Inbucket is an email testing service; it will accept messages for any email
address and make them available via web, REST and POP3 interfaces.  Once
compiled, Inbucket does not have any external dependencies - HTTP, SMTP, POP3
and storage are all built in.

A Go client for the REST API is available in
`github.com/inbucket/inbucket/pkg/rest/client` - [Go API docs]

Read more at the [Inbucket Website]

![Screenshot](http://www.inbucket.org/images/inbucket-ss1.png "Viewing a message")


## Development Status

Inbucket is currently production quality: it is being used for real work.

Please see the [Change Log] and [Issues List] for more details.  If you'd like
to contribute code to the project check out [CONTRIBUTING.md].


## Docker

Inbucket has automated [Docker Image] builds via Docker Hub.  The `latest` tag
tracks our tagged releases, and `edge` tracks our potentially unstable
`main` branch.

Start the docker image by running:

```
docker run -d --name inbucket -p 9000:9000 -p 2500:2500 -p 1100:1100 inbucket/inbucket
```

Then point your browser to [localhost:9000](http://localhost:9000/)

## Building from Source

You will need functioning [Go] and [Node.js] installations for this to work.

```sh
git clone https://github.com/inbucket/inbucket.git
cd inbucket/ui
yarn install
yarn build
cd ..
go build ./cmd/inbucket
```

For more information on building and development flows, check out the
[Development Quickstart] page of our wiki.

### Configure and Launch

Inbucket reads its configuration from environment variables, but comes with
reasonable defaults built-in.  It should work on most Unix and OS X machines as
is.  Launch the daemon:

```sh
./inbucket
```

By default the SMTP server will be listening on localhost port 2500 and
the web interface will be available at [localhost:9000](http://localhost:9000/).

See doc/[config.md] for more information on configuring Inbucket, but you will
likely find the [Configurator] tool the easiest way to generate a configuration.


## About

Inbucket is written in [Go] and [Elm].

Inbucket is open source software released under the MIT License.  The latest
version can be found at https://github.com/inbucket/inbucket

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Docker build file for Inbucket: https://www.inbucket.org/

### Build frontend
# Build frontend for amd64 due to architecture constraints for the Elm/Node frontend.
FROM node:20-alpine AS frontend

# Install build dependencies needed for node-gyp
RUN apk add --no-cache python3 make g++
RUN npm install -g node-gyp
WORKDIR /build
COPY . .
WORKDIR /build/ui
RUN rm -rf .parcel-cache dist elm-stuff node_modules
RUN yarn install --frozen-lockfile --non-interactive
RUN yarn run build

### Build backend
FROM golang:1.25-alpine3.22 AS backend
RUN apk add --no-cache --virtual .build-deps g++ git make
WORKDIR /build
COPY . .
ENV CGO_ENABLED=0
# Build with git describe if available; fall back to a static value when .git is not present
RUN set -eux; V=$(git describe --tags --always 2>/dev/null || echo "dev"); D=$(date -Iseconds); \
  go build -o inbucket -ldflags "-X 'main.version=$V' -X 'main.date=$D'" -v ./cmd/inbucket

### Run in minimal image
FROM alpine:3.22
RUN apk --no-cache add tzdata wget
WORKDIR /opt/inbucket
RUN mkdir bin defaults ui
COPY --from=backend /build/inbucket bin
COPY --from=frontend /build/ui/dist ui
COPY etc/docker/defaults/greeting.html defaults
COPY etc/docker/defaults/start-inbucket.sh /

iterations.injected_lessons:
1. **Git metadata dependency broke builds**  
   - **What happened:** Builds failed because `go build` used `git describe` for ldflags versioning, but `.git` wasn’t included in the Docker context (per Step 3).  
   - **Packages/commands:** `git describe`, Go build ldflags (`-X main.Version=`).  
   - **Fix:** Don’t rely on repo metadata during container builds. Retrieve version with `ARG VERSION` or bake a fallback (e.g., `-X main.Version=${VERSION:-unknown}`) so builds succeed without `.git`.  

2. **Frontend build missing Yarn**  
   - **What happened:** Step 11 fail: frontend stage used `RUN yarn install && yarn build` but Yarn wasn’t installed in the `node:20-alpine` image.  
   - **Packages/commands:** `yarn install`, `yarn build`.  
   - **Fix:** Install Yarn explicitly (`RUN corepack enable && corepack prepare yarn@stable --activate`) or switch to `npm ci && npm run build`.  

3. **Greeting file path mismatch**  
   - **What happened:** Step 13 fail: copied `defaults/greeting.html` into `/opt/inbucket/defaults/greeting.html`, but runtime env var `INBUCKET_WEB_GREETINGFILE` expected `/config/greeting.html`.  
   - **Packages/commands:** `COPY etc/docker/defaults/greeting.html …`, `ENV INBUCKET_WEB_GREETINGFILE=/config/greeting.html`.  
   - **Fix:** Either  
     - copy greeting file into `/config/greeting.html`, **or**  
     - change env var to `/opt/inbucket/defaults/greeting.html` consistent with copy path.  

4. **Non‑root security concern**  
   - **What happened:** Step 15 fail: container runs as root. Build didn’t create or `USER`‑switch to a lesser privileged account.  
   - **Fix:** In final stage add `RUN adduser -D inbucket` (Alpine) and `USER inbucket` before `CMD`. Ensure permissions for `/config` and `/opt/inbucket`.  

5. **Healthcheck configuration fragile**  
   - **What happened:** Step 15 fail: healthcheck depended on parsing `INBUCKET_WEB_ADDR`, which could vary by user override and cause failures.  
   - **Packages/commands:** `HEALTHCHECK CMD wget -qO- http://127.0.0.1:${WEB_PORT}/health`.  
   - **Fix:** Hardcode healthcheck against internal container port (e.g., `80` or `9000`) or dynamically extract port at runtime via entrypoint script.  

6. **Architecture constraint handling**  
   - **What happened:** Earlier steps indicated frontend builds must use x86 (`amd64`) images even when the final binary runs on arm64.  
   - **Fix:** Use a multi‑arch build: `FROM --platform=linux/amd64 node:20-alpine AS frontend` then compile Go in an arch‑neutral stage (`golang:1.25-alpine3.22`).  

7. **Best practices / reproducibility**  
   - **Fix:** Pin dependency versions (`node:20-alpine`, `golang:1.25-alpine3.22`), clean up caches (`--no-cache` on apk), and copy only needed build outputs to final stage.

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Multi-stage build for Inbucket

### Frontend (amd64 only)
FROM --platform=linux/amd64 node:current-alpine3.23 AS frontend
RUN npm install -g node-gyp
WORKDIR /build
COPY . .
WORKDIR /build/ui
RUN rm -rf .parcel-cache dist elm-stuff node_modules
RUN yarn install --frozen-lockfile --non-interactive
RUN yarn run build

### Backend
FROM golang:1.25-alpine3.22 AS backend
ARG VERSION=unknown
ARG BUILD_DATE=unknown
ENV VERSION=${VERSION}
ENV BUILD_DATE=${BUILD_DATE}
RUN apk add --no-cache --virtual .build-deps g++ git make
WORKDIR /build
COPY . .
ENV CGO_ENABLED=0
RUN go env -w GOPROXY=https://proxy.golang.org,direct
# Build with version metadata baked in (avoid git describe)
RUN go build -o inbucket -ldflags "-X 'main.version=${VERSION}' -X 'main.date=${BUILD_DATE}'" -v ./cmd/inbucket

### Runtime
FROM alpine:3.22
RUN apk --no-cache add tzdata wget

# Create non-root user for runtime
RUN adduser -D inbucket

WORKDIR /opt/inbucket

# Create required dirs and set permissions
RUN mkdir -p bin defaults ui /config /storage

COPY --from=backend /build/inbucket bin
COPY --from=frontend /build/ui/dist ui
COPY etc/docker/defaults/greeting.html /config/greeting.html
COPY etc/docker/defaults/start-inbucket.sh /start-inbucket.sh

# Configuration defaults
ENV INBUCKET_SMTP_DISCARDDOMAINS=bitbucket.local
ENV INBUCKET_SMTP_TIMEOUT=30s
ENV INBUCKET_POP3_TIMEOUT=30s
ENV INBUCKET_WEB_GREETINGFILE=/config/greeting.html
ENV INBUCKET_WEB_COOKIEAUTHKEY=secret-inbucket-session-cookie-key
ENV INBUCKET_WEB_UIDIR=ui
ENV INBUCKET_STORAGE_TYPE=file
ENV INBUCKET_STORAGE_PARAMS=path:/storage
ENV INBUCKET_STORAGE_RETENTIONPERIOD=72h
ENV INBUCKET_STORAGE_MAILBOXMSGCAP=300

# Healthcheck
HEALTHCHECK --interval=5s --timeout=5s --start-period=5s CMD wget -qO- http://127.0.0.1:9000/health || exit 1

# Ports: SMTP, HTTP, POP3
EXPOSE 2500 9000 1100

# Persistent Volumes
VOLUME /config
VOLUME /storage

# Ensure runtime ownership
RUN chown -R inbucket:inbucket /opt/inbucket/bin /opt/inbucket/ui /config /storage

USER inbucket
ENTRYPOINT ["/start-inbucket.sh"]
CMD ["-logjson"]
"}

steps.tool_output_raw:
#!/bin/sh
# start-inbucket.sh
# description: start inbucket (runs within a docker container)

INBUCKET_HOME="/opt/inbucket"
CONF_SOURCE="$INBUCKET_HOME/defaults"
CONF_TARGET="/config"

set -eo pipefail

install_default_config() {
  local file="$1"
  local source="$CONF_SOURCE/$file"
  local target="$CONF_TARGET/$file"

  if [ ! -e "$target" ]; then
    echo "Installing default $file to $CONF_TARGET"
    install "$source" "$target"
  fi
}

install_default_config "greeting.html"

exec "$INBUCKET_HOME/bin/inbucket" $*

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Multi-stage build for Inbucket

# Frontend (amd64) build using Node.js 20
FROM node:20-alpine AS frontend
RUN npm install -g node-gyp
RUN apk add --no-cache python3 make g++
WORKDIR /build
COPY . .

WORKDIR /build/ui
# Clean any cached artifacts
RUN rm -rf .parcel-cache dist elm-stuff node_modules
RUN corepack enable && corepack prepare yarn@stable --activate
RUN yarn install --frozen-lockfile --non-interactive
RUN yarn run build

### Backend
FROM golang:1.25-alpine3.22 AS backend
ARG VERSION=unknown
ARG BUILD_DATE=unknown
ENV VERSION=${VERSION}
ENV BUILD_DATE=${BUILD_DATE}
RUN apk add --no-cache --virtual .build-deps g++ git make
WORKDIR /build
COPY . .
ENV CGO_ENABLED=0
RUN go env -w GOPROXY=https://proxy.golang.org,direct
# Build with version metadata baked in (avoid git describe relying on .git)
RUN make clean deps
RUN go build -o inbucket -ldflags "-X 'main.version=${VERSION}' -X 'main.date=${BUILD_DATE}'" -v ./cmd/inbucket

### Runtime image
FROM alpine:3.22
RUN apk --no-cache add tzdata wget

# Non-root runtime user
RUN adduser -D inbucket

WORKDIR /opt/inbucket
RUN mkdir -p bin defaults ui /config /storage

COPY --from=backend /build/inbucket bin
COPY --from=frontend /build/ui/dist ui
COPY etc/docker/defaults/greeting.html /config/greeting.html
COPY etc/docker/defaults/start-inbucket.sh /start-inbucket.sh

# Ensure the startup script is executable
RUN chmod +x /start-inbucket.sh

# Configuration (defaults)
ENV INBUCKET_SMTP_DISCARDDOMAINS=bitbucket.local
ENV INBUCKET_SMTP_TIMEOUT=30s
ENV INBUCKET_POP3_TIMEOUT=30s
ENV INBUCKET_WEB_GREETINGFILE=/config/greeting.html
ENV INBUCKET_WEB_COOKIEAUTHKEY=secret-inbucket-session-cookie-key
ENV INBUCKET_WEB_UIDIR=ui
ENV INBUCKET_STORAGE_TYPE=file
ENV INBUCKET_STORAGE_PARAMS=path:/storage
ENV INBUCKET_STORAGE_RETENTIONPERIOD=72h
ENV INBUCKET_STORAGE_MAILBOXMSGCAP=300

# Healthcheck against internal port 9000
HEALTHCHECK --interval=5s --timeout=5s --start-period=5s CMD wget -qO- http://127.0.0.1:9000/health || exit 1

# Exposed ports
EXPOSE 2500 9000 1100

# Volumes
VOLUME /config
VOLUME /storage

# Ensure runtime ownership and non-root execution
RUN chown -R inbucket:inbucket /opt/inbucket/bin /opt/inbucket/ui /config /storage
USER inbucket
ENTRYPOINT ["/start-inbucket.sh"]
CMD ["-logjson"]
"}

runs.final_dockerfile:
# Docker build file for Inbucket: https://www.inbucket.org/

### Build frontend
FROM node:20 AS frontend
RUN npm install -g node-gyp
WORKDIR /build
COPY . .
WORKDIR /build/ui
RUN rm -rf .parcel-cache dist elm-stuff node_modules
RUN yarn install --frozen-lockfile --non-interactive
RUN yarn run build

### Build backend
FROM golang:1.25-alpine3.22 AS backend
RUN apk add --no-cache --virtual .build-deps g++ git make
WORKDIR /build
COPY . .
ENV CGO_ENABLED=0
RUN make clean deps
RUN go build -o inbucket \
  -ldflags "-X 'main.version=$(git describe --tags --always)' -X 'main.date=$(date -Iseconds)'" \
  -v ./cmd/inbucket

### Run in minimal image
FROM alpine:3.22
RUN apk --no-cache add tzdata
# healthcheck utility
RUN apk --no-cache add wget
WORKDIR /opt/inbucket
RUN mkdir bin defaults ui
COPY --from=backend /build/inbucket bin
COPY --from=frontend /build/ui/dist ui
COPY etc/docker/defaults/greeting.html defaults
COPY etc/docker/defaults/start-inbucket.sh /start-inbucket.sh

# Configuration
ENV INBUCKET_SMTP_DISCARDDOMAINS=bitbucket.local
ENV INBUCKET_SMTP_TIMEOUT=30s
ENV INBUCKET_POP3_TIMEOUT=30s
ENV INBUCKET_WEB_GREETINGFILE=/config/greeting.html
ENV INBUCKET_WEB_COOKIEAUTHKEY=secret-inbucket-session-cookie-key
ENV INBUCKET_WEB_UIDIR=ui
ENV INBUCKET_STORAGE_TYPE=file
ENV INBUCKET_STORAGE_PARAMS=path:/storage
ENV INBUCKET_STORAGE_RETENTIONPERIOD=72h
ENV INBUCKET_STORAGE_MAILBOXMSGCAP=300

# Healthcheck
HEALTHCHECK --interval=5s --timeout=5s --retries=3 CMD /bin/sh -c 'wget localhost:$(echo ${INBUCKET_WEB_ADDR:-0.0.0.0:9000}|cut -d: -f2) -q -O - >/dev/null'

# Ports: SMTP, HTTP, POP3
EXPOSE 2500 9000 1100

# Persistent Volumes
VOLUME /config
VOLUME /storage

ENTRYPOINT ["/start-inbucket.sh"]
CMD ["-logjson"]
